docs.title: Technical Writing Portfolio
docs.text: Welcome to my technical documentation portfolio. Here you will find examples of API references, installation guides, and developer documentation designed to make complex systems easy to understand.

docs.title: Professional Overview
docs.text:
I am a Technical Writer and Localization Specialist with a strong linguistic background (English C2, French C2) and hands-on experience in Python, Git, and Data Structures (JSON, XML).

My approach focuses on the Docs-as-Code methodology: managing documentation using the same tools and workflows as software development.

docs.title: 1. WeatherAPI Integration Guide
docs.text: A comprehensive guide on how to integrate a REST API, authenticate requests, and handle JSON responses using Python. * Target Audience: Frontend and Backend Developers * Tools Used: Markdown, OpenAPI/Swagger, Python, JSON

docs.title: 2. Markdown & Git Cheat Sheet (Coming Soon)
docs.text: A quick-start guide for internal development teams to standardize technical writing workflows.

docs.title: API Error Handling Guide
docs.text: When integrating with the WeatherAPI, developers may encounter errors due to invalid requests, authentication failures, or server-side issues. This guide outlines the standard HTTP status codes, error codes, and the structure of error responses returned by our API.

docs.title: HTTP Status Codes
docs.text: We use standard HTTP response codes to indicate the success or failure of an API request.

docs.title: 1. Client-Side Errors (4xx)
docs.text:
These status codes indicate that there was an issue with the request sent by the client (e.g., missing parameters, invalid key, or incorrect URL structure).

HTTP Status Error Name Common Cause How to Fix 400 Bad Request The city name query parameter q is empty or invalid. Verify that the q parameter is not empty and contains a valid location name. 401 Unauthorized The API key parameter key is invalid or missing. Check your dashboard to ensure you are passing the correct API key. 403 Forbidden Your API key does not have access to this resource. Upgrade your subscription plan or check your API key permissions. 404 Not Found The requested endpoint does not exist. Double-check the URL path for typos (e.g., /v1/current.json). 429 Too Many Requests The API key has exceeded its monthly request limit. Upgrade your subscription plan or implement local caching to reduce requests.

docs.title: 2. Server-Side Errors (5xx)
docs.text:
These status codes indicate that something went wrong on the Weather API servers. The developer's request was correct, but the system is temporarily down.

HTTP Status Error Name Common Cause How to Fix 500 Internal Error An unexpected error occurred on the weather server. Retry the request after a few seconds. If the issue persists, contact support. 503 Service Unavailable The server is temporarily offline for maintenance. Wait a few minutes and try the request again.

docs.title: Error Response Structure (JSON)
docs.text:
In the event of an error, the API returns a detailed error description in JSON format to help developers diagnose the issue.

JSON Error Example (HTTP 400 Bad Request)

{
  "error": {
    "code": 1003,
    "message": "Parameter 'q' is missing.",
    "type": "invalid_request_error"
  }
}

docs.title: Response Fields
docs.text: Field Name Type Description error.code Integer Internal WeatherAPI unique error identifier. error.message String A human-readable description of what went wrong. error.type String Categorization of the error (e.g., invalid_request_error or authentication_error).

docs.title: Weather API Integration Guide
docs.text: This guide explains how to integrate with the WeatherAPI service to retrieve real-time weather data using Python.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: The WeatherAPI allows developers to fetch current weather conditions by sending HTTP GET requests. The API responses are returned in JSON format.

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
To use the API, you must include your personal API key in the request URL as a query parameter named key.

Example Request URL: 

https://api.weatherapi.com/v1/current.json?key=YOUR_API_KEY&q=Paris

docs.title: Code Example (Python)
docs.text:
Below is a complete Python script using the requests library to fetch current weather data.

import requests

def get_current_weather(api_key, city):
    url = "https://api.weatherapi.com/v1/current.json"
    params = {
        "key": api_key,
        "q": city,
        "aqi": "no"
    }

    try:
        response = requests.get(url, params=params)
        response.raise_for_status() 
        data = response.json()
        return data

    except requests.exceptions.HTTPError as error:
        print(f"HTTP Error occurred: {error}")
        return None

# Usage example:
# weather_data = get_current_weather("your_key_here", "London")

docs.title: Response Structure
docs.text: A successful request returns an HTTP 200 OK status code and a JSON object. Here is an example of the response body and its field definitions.

docs.title: Field Definitions
docs.text: Field Path Type Description Example location.name String The name of the requested city or location. "London" location.country String The country where the location is situated. "United Kingdom" current.temp_c Float Current temperature in Celsius. 15.0 current.condition.text String Weather condition text description. "Partly cloudy" current.wind_kph Float Wind speed measured in kilometers per hour. 11.2 `
